text:
Kacho Hui Guo Rou (カチョウ＝ホイコーロ, Kachō Hoikōro) was the Tenth Prince of the Kakin Empire's fourteen Princes, first of Kakin's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou and his sixth wife, Sixth Queen Seiko Hui Guo Rou's two daughters. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 She was the elder twin sister of 11 th Prince Fugetsu Hui Guo Rou. 

Appearance

Kacho was a relatively slender girl with large, fair, shoulder-length blonde hair, long eyelashes, and black eyes. She dresses rather flashily compared to her relatively plain younger twin sister, Fugetsu, but not as flamboyantly as her eldest half-sister Camilla.

In Kacho's first appearance, she wore a plain dark-colored dress with a flowery collar. During the eve of departure festival, she wore a matching suit and skirt with Fugetsu. Later that day on the Black Whale, she wore an elegant dress with a large ribbon at chest height and an ornate yet relatively simple necklace. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 She wore the same dress to the departure ceremony the next day, but with a different necklace. She and Fugetsu matched outfits once again at the banquet, turning up a light-colored top with a brooch below the collar, changing into matching light-colored dresses for a meal (where Fugetsu also wore a hairband), and finally adding flowers in their hair for their performance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

Personality

As noted by Melody, Kacho was terrifying and takes on a convincing shallow, spoiled, and generally detestable persona. She chooses her bodyguards due to their appearances, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 but, unlike Tyson, she chooses ugly ones. Despite her status as a junior prince, she is shown to be cold-blooded, intending to form an alliance with Fugetsu even before the Succession War starts to murder her other siblings, promising her that when she becomes the new ruler, she will request her father to spare Fugetsu. 

However, Melody claims that Kacho lies to herself, and that her bratty, cold façade is due to the expectations placed on her, as well as a way to make sure that, if forced to choose between saving her and her sister, her employees will save the latter. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 In reality, she resents being dragged into a fight to the death with her own family and cares about her twin sister deeply, crying while looking at photos of the two of them spending time together. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 

She is fond of jewelry. 

Background

When they were children, Fugetsu and Kacho would often play together in a playground called "Magic Worm". One of their favorite games was pretending to travel the world, and Kacho would always let Fugetsu decide their next destination. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Kacho is first officially seen when Kakin's 14 Princes are introduced. Kacho is seen admiring a ring on her right hand, next to an intricate necklace in a glass display case. Kacho is seen wearing a simple black long-sleeved shirt, with a slightly fancier collar than Fugetsu, wearing a similar outfit. Kacho is one of six Princes to put up anonymous listings for bodyguards on Kakin's website. Melody applies to her listing and is contracted to become her bodyguard for the trip to the Dark Continent. 

During the eve (of the departure) festival, Kacho is seen with the rest of her siblings and Beyond on stage wearing a black suit with a skirt, holding hands with Fugetsu, and waving to the crowd. Kakin's Royal Family is flown into the Black Whale 1 shortly after, where they reside on Tier 1 during the journey to the New Continent. Kacho is next seen attending a departure ceremony taking place in the ballroom on Tier 1 of the Black Whale 1 in a strapless light-colored dress and an ornate necklace, attending to guests together with Fugetsu. 

On the day of departure, she attends a lunch banquet at the ceremonial hall wearing a similar dress with a simpler necklace. She is notified of her time to retire from the banquet by her father's personal butler. She insists that Fugetsu accompany her to the connecting corridor despite his reminder that only one Prince is to leave at a time. There, she asks Fugetsu to form an alliance, saying that once she wins the Succession War, she will ask her father to spare Fugetsu. She then leaves with her bodyguards, complaining about the perverted stares of an old man from the RG Foundation. Melody eavesdrops on her conversation and forms an initial impression about her employer, but listens to Kacho's heartbeat and realizes her bratty facade is a lie. 

Several hours after returning to Room 1010 from the lunch banquet, she knocks a tray of food to the floor, calling it disgusting and demanding that it be remade. Melody's advanced hearing allows her to hear Kacho's bodyguards and maids complaining about her in whispers. Melody worries that this act she believes Kacho is putting on, if done to protect herself, would backfire. 

Later that night, Kacho is contacted by Room 1014 and agrees to send two representatives to learn Nen under Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 She sends her servants Loberry and Yuri. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 The next morning, during Nen lessons in Room 1014, an incident with Loberry involving the death of fellow attendee Barrigen Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 leads to an investigator sent to Room 1010 to monitor it for 72 hours; the incident not being serious enough to charge a member of the royal family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 Kacho is seen reading a book while listening to music through headphones as her mother, 6 th Queen Seiko, gets questioned by the investigator. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

Late that night, at about 1:30 a.m. on the third day, Kacho cries in bed while looking at photos of herself with her twin sister. Suddenly, a hatch appears in her bed and opens, and Kacho is shocked to see Fugetsu cautiously poking her head through. Kacho quickly collects herself and motions for Fugetsu to quickly close the hatch and hide. Melody hears a change in Kacho's heartbeat and rushes to her side. The two have an exchange where Kacho stubbornly refuses Melody's gentle attempts at coaxing her to open up, and Melody determines that Kacho should know the truth, and asks Kacho if she could talk to her about Nen. 

Later that day, Kacho orders Melody to help her with her studies. While seemingly reprimanding her bodyguard, she uses a high-frequency device to communicate with her in Morse code, instructing her on how to find one herself. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

The next day, Kacho tells her mother that she wants to put up a performance during the Sunday banquet. She and Fugetsu would play the glass harp, and Melody would play the flute. She asks Seiko to tell Nasubi and Fugetsu about it and Seiko agrees, saying she would make a second appeal to Nasubi. It is unclear why this is a second appeal Right before Seiko leaves, Kacho confidently reminds her of her resolve to fight with dignity and pride, bringing tears to Seiko's eyes. After Seiko leaves, Kacho stomps away with the investigator, making a scene to hide her secret communications to Melody through the Mosquitone. Kacho tells Melody that they would likely perform at the banquet, that she would arrange for Melody and Keeney to stand by in a specific location, and that she would arrange to meet with Fugetsu. Melody tells Kacho to make sure that the speakers hear her performance throughout Tier 1 and that she would investigate routes. Melody quietly watches Kacho as she directs her bodyguards and servants to set up a table and bring out the glass harp. 

On the fourth day, Kacho lies on her bed and reads a fashion magazine with earbuds in while Melody demonstrates her skills in a small performance to Seiko and the investigator. Seiko is moved, and excitedly ensures to arrange that Melody's performance would be broadcast across Tier 1. Kacho and Melody exchange a small nod. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 

Escape Attempt

On the eighth day of the voyage, Kacho attends the banquet with her sister and Melody. A few schedule changes occur but the twins remain the last to perform, as they had hoped. Right before executing their plan, Kacho reassures her nervous sister, stating that she never thinks about failure. They wear their headphones, with Kacho explaining to the overseeing guard that they were rehearsing their piece. Melody's performance entrances everyone in the upper tiers, and Keeney leads the twins to the lifeboats. They thank him and board one. As they approach the exit, Kacho promises her sister that they will never be apart and that all they need is each other. Kacho suddenly has a terrible feeling, and ghostly hands manifest all around them as soon as they leave the Black Whale. Kacho realizes that escaping the succession contest means death and urges Fugetsu to summon her magic door to return to the ship. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383 

Fugetsu makes it inside the tunnel and then sees the door closed behind her. Almost immediately after, she sees the door reopen, with Kacho smiling and crawling in. Unbeknownst to her, Kacho had remained on the other side and died, and her Guardian Spirit Beast's ability (Without You) is activated, manifesting as Kacho herself (hereon referred to as "Kacho"), who had been the one to open the door again. Together, the sisters return to Fugetsu's room. 

In a flashback, as Theta prepares to assassinate 4 th Prince Tserriednich, a surprise performance from the royal banquet for Tier 1 and Tier 2 is announced, with Melody of the Hunter Association and Princes Kacho and Fugetsu as performers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 385 

After the incident at the banquet, Melody sits in the Justice Bureau on Tier 2. The investigator checks on her, informing her of the suicide note that Keeney left saying he acted alone in the twins' escape attempt. As he continues speaking, Melody realizes that she is being kept to protect her from the dangerous Princes. She asks about the twins, and he says that they are also being questioned and that they said Keeney forced them onto the lifeboat. He adds that he has no evidence to prove or disprove if they were forced or if it was their own idea, and so would continue questioning them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

After Kurapika explains the details of learning Nen to Yuri, he adds that he hopes the battle for succession has no more victims and asks her to see if Kacho and Seiko would be willing to send more people to another round of classes. Yuri agrees to ask, and Kurapika thanks her. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 388 

2 nd Prince Camilla's guards are introduced and revealed to be curse assassins, with Mozbe, one of them, assigned to curse Kacho. Sarahell considers activating a curse on Kacho, Fugetsu, or 13 th Prince Marayam to test the efficiency of their curse, and during discussion hears that from Room 1002, Mozbe's curse would take ten days to activate, and also that Kacho and Fugetsu are being held for an escape attempt, adding with rumors of fatalities. Sarahell worries about their rooms becoming sealed, and Fukataki points out that their curses will take more than six months if they are in the cells. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Outside Room 1010, two guards stand in front of the doors as four soldiers surround them, waiting to enter. Inside, Seiko uses the phone and tells them to hold off on the regulations until she gets her personal belongings organized, demanding to know why she could not see Fugetsu despite being a Queen and her mother. She again expresses her concern for Fugetsu and tells them that until then, cordoning off the room is unacceptable. Under her breath, she contemplates letting others think Kacho is alive as long as possible, and then loudly informs the soldiers that she will speak to Fugetsu when the time is right, insisting that they must go through her, and then hanging up the phone. She then says to herself that while Kacho dropping out so early is a heavy loss, she hated the way her daughter looked at her and spoke to her like she knew everything. She adds that she was an important shield for Fugetsu and says that while she can control the latter, she's too weak to face the succession contest on her own, hoping that she can stay under the Justice Bureau's protection for as long as possible. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 

Without You

Since Without You had been activated, "Kacho" persists in coming up with a strategy to save her sister. At the Justice Bureau's interrogation office, "Kacho" speaks with Melody and the investigator about how she was possibly resurrected as a Guardian Spirit Beast to protect Fugetsu. "Kacho" points out that although someone protected with a Guardian Spirit Beast is unable to see the Guardian Spirit Beast of other people, Fugetsu could see her current form. She also wonders if Fugetsu's aura is being used to maintain her appearance, considerably burdening her, and begins feeling responsible for her weakened condition, wondering if finding out the truth will reduce the burden. Melody blames herself for what happened, but "Kacho" orders her to pull herself together and to feel bad about it when she is alone. 

"Kacho" reassures Melody that this is what she wanted, and declares that they will make Fugetsu King and that if they could not escape, they would fight. She suggests that the fact that everyone thinks she is dead, lets them have her suddenly turning up alive as an advantage, but adds that they need to keep her death a secret from Fugetsu. The investigator tells "Kacho" that she should let Fugetsu know that the people are under the mistaken impression that she died, confident that Fugetsu will accept the explanation since she exists. The investigator then tells Melody that she could not disappoint a young girl who is so determined and waiting for her. "Kacho" asks Melody if she is in or out, and Melody agrees to help them. 

The investigator starts with Melody's problem and reveals that five Princes have requested to see her, that the Third and Fourth Princes wish to speak with her in their quarters and to hear her performance in person, and after that the First, Fifth, and Seventh Princes more transparently offered a "pardon" in exchange for an "encore" of her performance. "Kacho" wonders what he means by a pardon, but Melody signals for him to not to say anything about Keeney's death. The investigator's explanation helps "Kacho" realize that the Princes want Melody's Nen abilities to indirectly help with assassinations, and nonchalantly says that it will lead to fewer Princes. 

Melody is taken aback by how harsh "Kacho" is. She explains some details of her ability, and after discussion, the investigator says that the best way to protect Fugetsu is to deceive all the Princes who have requested a performance, suggesting that they should wipe out all the higher-ranking Princes themselves. 

After a discussion with Melody, the investigator tells "Kacho" to return to the interrogation room. While phasing through a bookcase, "Kacho" asks him if he should interrogate her a little, but he replies that he is more worried about Fugetsu, telling "Kacho" that everyone would think she remained silent anyway. He leaves with Melody and meets "Kacho" in the other room, escorting her out while keeping in mind that the VIP witness protection area is the safest on the ship and that not even the First Prince could try anything there. 

Fugetsu's Condition

"Kacho" returns to their quarters at the Justice Bureau and calls for Fugetsu but hears no response. Aware that they are not allowed to leave their room because they are key witnesses and worried that Fugetsu used her ability to go somewhere else, "Kacho" checks the bed and removes the comforter to find Fugetsu's outgoing door. Upon seeing it, "Kacho" begins to worry further, aware that she was Fugetsu's return, and wonders if Fugetsu is still inside the tunnel, possibly using her ability to hide from an attacker, perhaps from Kaiser, the Justice Bureau official. Fugetsu suddenly opens the door, and "Kacho" quickly grabs her hands and asks if she's okay. Fugetsu excitedly replies that she has made a breakthrough, revealing that while she could only use the ability once a day before, she can now use it multiple times a day. With heavy bags under eyes, she adds that the return door now appears even when she's alone and that even though she can only return to where she was, she can now explore on her own even when Kacho is resting. 

"Kacho" wonders if she is really okay, and Fugetsu excitedly assures her that she is, adding that she feels happy and empowered every time she uses her magic, hoping that the two of them can explore and use their magic skills to escape together. Left wondering if the ability really gets stronger with every use, "Kacho" grabs her sister's shoulders and tells her to take it easy since she's so pale. However, Fugetsu grabs "Kacho"'s wrist as tears stream down her face, and she tells "Kacho" that it's her turn to protect her since she has been doing it for so many years. Taken aback, "Kacho" suggests that they rest and talk about it once they wake up. "Kacho" then meets with Melody and reveals that Fugetsu passed out, "Kacho" that it's not normal, and says that she can't figure out what's happening. Eager to help, Melody reminds her that she is also a key witness and won't be able to get anywhere near the two of them. She tells Kacho that she will have Kaiser arrange a time for her to meet with the other Princes and for Fugetsu to be interviewed so that they can pass each other in the hallway. Melody concluded that Fugetsu is under the influence of evil power and needs to be exorcised. 

At 6:00 a.m. on the eleventh day, "Kacho" pops herself through the ceiling of Melody's cell and shows her a picture of a mark on Fugetsu's right shoulder blade, which they did not have before their escape. She describes it as a bug bite, and Melody suggests it could be an injection site. "Kacho" says that it was not possible since they were brought to the Justice Bureau right after they went back to their room, making Melody revise her guess to be a mark made by Nen as part of a multi-step Nen ability and probably due to Fugetsu falling into a trap. Melody asks "Kacho" if Fugetsu had said something weird yesterday, and "Kacho" begins to have a bad feeling while recalling Fugetsu's excitement at being able to use Magical Worm multiple times in a day. "Kacho" asks Melody to clarify if she means that an enemy ability is the reason for Fugetsu's belief that she could use her ability multiple times in a day. Melody tells her to convince Fugetsu to stop using Magical Worm. Still confused, "Kacho" asks if Fugetsu would really need persuasion to stop once she knows it is not her ability. Melody says that she hopes not, and that Fugetsu is showing signs of addiction. "Kacho" is upset to hear that and asks if Fugetsu has been injected with some sort of drug, calling it a terrible thing. Melody says that she could not think of any other explanation and that none of the princes who came to see her were responsible for Fugetsu's condition before she suggested finding out who was responsible. Kacho has an idea of who it could be. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 

They continue their conversation a short while later with Kaiser in his office, where "Kacho" suggests that Luzurus was responsible, corroborated by the fact that he sells drugs to back the Cha-R Family. She suggests that it was the work of his Guardian Spirit Beast, then Melody points out that convincing him of what he was doing was impossible because he would not be conscious of its actions. "Kacho" says that the only way to stop Fugetsu's condition is to defeat either Luzurus or his Guardian Spirit Beast, and concludes that they should take action immediately. She shares that she already has a plan and lets them know that Fugetsu has been starting to test her ability; Fugetsu is unable to travel off the ship but can access the inside of an un-launched lifeboat, and wants to test if people other than them can enter the tunnel. Melody says that she would need someone to carry an unconscious Luzurus into the tunnel, and "Kacho" agrees, wishing that Luzurus would be stupid enough to get on a lifeboat himself. "Kacho" says that Melody's ability would guarantee he enters one, and that it might be even better if they could poison or strangle him on the spot. Kaiser disagrees with her last point and says that it would be better to make it look like an accident and volunteer to be the person to carry him through the tunnel before suggesting that they carry out their plan. At the same time, they are getting questioned so that they could use the questioning as an alibi. "Kacho" and Melody exchange a look, and Kaiser elaborates with a plausible excuse using the fact that Luzurus and his guards did drugs. 

After listening to Kaiser's suggestions, "Kacho" asks Melody for her thoughts. Melody says that the plan sounds alright, but asks where Magical Worm's door would connect. "Kacho" and Kaiser look at Melody, and after Kaiser does not respond, "Kacho" says that it would need to connect to Luzurus' master bedroom, meaning that Fugetsu would need to go there in-person. Kaiser does not understand, so they explain that Fugetsu must have been to a place before being able to travel there with Magical Worm. Kaiser volunteers to arrange for this and asks them to come up with a reason why she needs to meet with each Prince. "Kacho" catches on to his intent and checks if he meant to suggest visiting more Princes than just Luzurus. Kaiser explained that maximizing the places she could visit was best and that it could be useful later. She agrees and suggests writing a letter containing her last message to each Prince. Kaiser reasons it out and agrees with it. "Kacho" points out that some Princes might not let them in and personally accept the letter, whether because Fugetsu was a lower-ranked Prince or because they had something to hide, like Luzurus likely did. As Kaiser imagines a way of doing so, "Kacho" and Melody look at him. He looks up to see them staring at him and asks if he has already explained why he was helping them so much. "Kacho" and Melody watch him as he shares his vision for the world, one where Fugetsu took the throne, where having "Kacho" beside her could protect her from their mother's meddling. 

Fugetsu is escorted by Kaiser and the Ministry of Justice to Tier 1's VVIP area, where they explain to the guards stationed outside Benjamin's residence that Kacho had passed away but had written letters to be personally handed to each prince in the event of her death. After Fugetsu enters, Benjamin sees the spirits plaguing her and guesses that she was despondent after Kacho's death. Fugetsu next visits and passes a letter to Tubeppa and 6 th Prince Tyson each before proceeding to Luzurus' Room 1007. Luzurus apologizes to Fugetsu about Kacho, and Fugetsu thanks him for his kind words as she passes "Kacho"'sletter to him. He begins reading it and asks Fugetsu if Kacho had told her anything about its contents or if all the letters were the same. Fugetsu says that Kacho never had a chance to tell her, and Luzurus guesses that it is because she did not want to involve Fugetsu. He decides to pass the letter to Kaiser, who pretends to read it, having been the person who told "Kacho" what to write in the first place. 

Kaiser thinks back to their earlier conversation, where he suggested turning "Kacho"'s letter into an information "bomb" containing information that the Ministry of Justice had gathered on the Princes. Melody asks how "Kacho" would have known the information that the Ministry of Justice had gathered. Kaiser says that he would be present as Fugetsu delivered the letter to help deflect questions that dug too deep into Kacho, and says that he did not intend for her to return to Tier 1 until the succession contest was over. 

As Kaiser reads the letter, Luzurus contemplates the odd feeling he had about its contents. He wonders why Kacho's last message wasn't to ask for protection for Fugetsu, but instead had bombshells about the other princes. He thinks that because Kacho is smart, each letter has different contents and could be a way to get him and Tserriednich to fight while Kacho goes to Benjamin and gets his help to protect Fugetsu. 

At 8:50 a.m., after Fugetsu returned to Tier 2 and promptly fell asleep, "Kacho", Melody, and Kaiser gathered to continue discussing their plans. Since Fugetsu had successfully visited Room 1007, "Kacho" suggests beginning as soon as possible, when Fugetsu wakes up, so that they have time to try something else if getting rid of Luzurus did not address her condition. "Kacho" restates that they would have a perfect alibi if Kaiser had done the questioning while Fugetsu was being treated in the sick bay and guards were placed outside to vouch that they never left the room. She takes a back seat, watches as Melody thinks, and ensures their plan works. Melody brings up the fact that the only Princes to reject their letters were Tserriednich and Marayam, though Halkenburg had also been unable to receive his letter due to him being under medical treatment. Melody also reassures her that, thanks to Basho's help, the evil spirits stay away from her even when she is by herself, and suggests that they let Fugetsu sleep and get some rest, as she needs to recover her strength. Kacho agrees and says that it would be the worst if Fugetsu could not use her ability at a critical moment. Kaiser suggests they get some rest, too, and "Kacho" declares that they will begin their plans the next day. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

"Kacho" spends the rest of the eleventh day seated with Fugetsu as she rests in the infirmary, watching over her. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 Here, "Kacho"'s body is drawn translucently. It is currently unclear whether or not this is a stylistic choice or indicative of something like Fugetsu's Nen, which "Kacho" suspects powers her, weakening. 

Kacho's Employees

Equipment

Mosquitone Device: Kacho owns a communication device that produces high-pitched sounds, similar to the buzzing of a mosquito, that only people below a certain age can hear. It is activated with the press of one's thumb and is used to send coded messages, for example, through Morse code. 

Abilities & Powers

Despite being a Kakin royal family member, according to Tubeppa, Kacho has no real political influence or power. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 She is a very capable actress who can convincingly keep up her spoiled persona for prolonged lengths of time with only the sound of her heartbeat giving her away, and also while sending messages in code at the same time. She is capable of thinking ahead, which she demonstrated by acquiring a special communication device that adults cannot hear. She knows Morse code and can play the glass harp. 

Morse Code Knowledge: Kacho secretly communicated with Melody using Morse code while maintaining a fake conversation. 

Guardian Spirit Beast

Like the rest of her siblings, Kacho received an egg from which a Guardian Spirit Beast, a variety of Nen beast, hatched to protect her. As a parasitic-type ability, she has no control over it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 The Guardian Spirit Beast is subjected to two rule-like instincts: it is unable to fight other Guardian Spirit Beasts as well as to directly attack the host of one. 

Much is still unknown about how Without You works as a Guardian Spirit Beast. Its initial explanation reveals that Without You takes the form of the first twin to die, to protect the other and stay by her side until her death. 

When Kacho dies, Without You assumes her form and stays by Fugetsu's side as she returns to Room 1011, demonstrating the ability to interact with Fugetsu's Magical Worm as Kacho would normally have been able to in the process. In addition, it acquires some form of intangibility, able to walk through walls while not falling through the ground, and able to seemingly stand on a ceiling. Without You becomes visible to others after taking on Kacho's form, even to Fugetsu, who should normally be unable to see neither Nen nor a Guardian Spirit Beast. Without You is also seen to be aware of the fact that it is visible and that Guardian Spirit Beasts should not generally be visible. 

Without You suggests that it has resurrected as a "guardian spirit" to protect Fugetsu, and speculates that it may have been her(Kacho's) Guardian Spirit Beast's ability that did so, demonstrating that it had awareness of the events leading up to Kacho's death. This new Kacho seemingly retains all her mental faculties, including personality, memory, logical intelligence, and general consciousness. Nothing is currently known about its capabilities or level of sentience before taking Kacho's form, it demonstrated no physical existence in the story before then and the only piece of evidence suggesting any form of existence is the fact that Kacho was assumed to have undergone the Seed Urn ceremony like her siblings.

Manga Appearances

Quotes

(To Melody) "It's your job, so just do your best and get paid. Just wander around and pretend that you're worried about me, like you did now." 
(To Seiko) "This is a holy battle to decide the glorious king of Kakin. I will fight with dignity and pride. It's nothing to be scared or sad about." 
(To Fugetsu) "We'll always be together! We can make it with the two of us!" 

Trivia

"Kachōfūgetsu" (花鳥風月) is a Japanese saying that is literally translatable as "Flower, Bird, Wind, Moon" and adapted means "The Beauty of Nature" or "Beauties of Nature". It is an invitation to behold the wonders of nature and, in doing so, learn about oneself.
During an interview, Togashi mentioned that Without You was his favorite Nen ability. Togashi revealed that while he was drawing storyboards, he was so moved when he saw the choreographer of his favorite idol group talking about the intention behind the choreography of Season of Two and his interpretation of the song that he finished the storyboards at once. In that same interview, he mentioned that the ability he would like to use most was Magical Worm, to save him travel time and expenses, and to crawl to a hospital if his lower back happened to go out during the trip. Togashi’s Q&A on “Iwakura and Yoshizumi’s Show” 

Intertextuality and References

The second name of her Guardian Spirit Beast's ability, "Season of Two" (Futari Saison), is a reference to the third single by the Japanese girl idol group Keyakizaka46 and the main name, "Without You", comes from the fourth track (Kimi ga Inai) from the "Masshirona Mono wa Yogoshitaku Naru" music album of the same idol group.

Translations around the World
